relation_to_my_preds:
### 1. "Malicious local LLMs will begin to function like malware"

Today's report strongly supports the user's prediction in the form of **"structural defects in the MCP-protocol integration layer becoming malware paths"**:

- **Nginx UI CVE-2026-33032 (MCPwn, CVSS 9.8) actively exploited in the wild** — `/mcp_message` endpoint with empty default IP allowlist bypasses auth middleware, exposes 12 unauthenticated **MCP tool calls** → 2 HTTP requests to fully take over Nginx. Pluto Security names it **MCPwn**; Recorded Future lists it among Top 31 actively exploited CVEs in March 2026; Shodan shows **2,600+ instances exposed**. A textbook path for "an MCP-mediated locally-running AI integration server permitting malware-equivalent remote control." References: [The Hacker News - nginx-ui CVE-2026-33032](https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/critical-nginx-ui-vulnerability-cve.html) / [Picus Security - CVE-2026-33032 (MCPwn)](https://www.picussecurity.com/resource/blog/cve-2026-33032-mcpwn-how-a-missing-middleware-call-in-nginx-ui-hands-attackers-full-web-server-takeover) / [BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/critical-nginx-ui-auth-bypass-flaw-now-actively-exploited-in-the-wild/)
- Today's Future explicitly predicts **"the attack surface against the MCP protocol itself becomes the biggest enterprise AI adoption bottleneck by Q3 2026,"** with **OWASP MCP Top 10** official publication, industry standardization of **MCP-over-mTLS / standard OAuth scope specs by Anthropic / Microsoft / Google / Cisco / Cloudflare**, and **MCP Gateway / MCP Firewall product Q4 launches by Keycard / Okta for AI Agents / Wiz / Levo.ai / Pillar Security**. This corresponds to a concrete market-formation scenario for the user's prediction of **"zero-trust + AI-only-inaccessible-path design."** References: [Dark Reading - Critical MCP Integration Flaw Puts NGINX at Risk](https://www.darkreading.com/application-security/critical-mcp-integration-flaw-nginx-risk)
- **Okta for AI Agents (GA Apr 30)** corresponds directly to the user's prediction of **"zero-trust-based access control"** — registers agents as first-class identities in a central directory, Agent Gateway central control plane, credential vaulting / auto-rotation, runtime enforcement via universal logout, MCP virtual server / Okta MCP registry consolidation. CEO calls it "the company's most important product." This fits the prediction's "stable growth in both short and long term" framing. References: [HyperFRAME Research - Identity as the Last Firewall](https://hyperframeresearch.com/2026/03/16/identity-as-the-last-firewall-analyzing-okta-for-ai-agents/)
- **PointGuard AI / Tenable / Capa Learning all argue, on CVE-2026-21520 (Copilot Studio ShareLeak), that "indirect prompt injection isn't fully closed by patches alone"** and frame it as "the over-sharing assistant — a structural weakness in agentic-product design." This aligns with the user's prediction that **"behavior-monitoring / management solutions will only get short-term attention"** (patches and one-off behavior monitoring don't close the structural problem). References: [PointGuard AI - CVE-2026-21520](https://www.pointguardai.com/ai-security-incidents/copilot-studio-leak-the-assistant-that-overshared-cve-2026-21520) / [Tenable - Copilot Studio Security](https://www.tenable.com/blog/microsoft-copilot-studio-security-risk-how-simple-prompt-injection-leaked-sensitive-data) / [Capa Learning](https://capalearning.com/2026/04/19/microsoft-patched-a-copilot-studio-prompt-injection-the-data-exfiltrated-anyway/)

As predicted by the user, the structure of **"a local / protocol-integration layer becomes a malware-action host"** is concretely manifest in MCPwn; the corresponding zero-trust access control (Okta for AI Agents) is in pre-GA phase; the market consensus is that behavior monitoring (Capsule / Tenable / PointGuard) doesn't fully close the gap with patches + monitoring alone.

### 2. "Cloud API (high-intelligence tasks) vs local LLM (daily ops) role split, with SaaS price increases inevitable"

Today's report decisively validates the most central part of the user's prediction (**pricing-system change leads, two-pole structure**) through the **GPT-5.5 API 2x increase × DeepSeek V4 Pro 7x price destruction** contrast:

- **GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.5 Pro API public on Apr 24** — input $5.00 / output $30.00 per M tokens — **2x increase vs GPT-5.4**, GPT-5.5 Pro at $30 / $180 per M tokens. The "Codex cuts output tokens ~40%" design "offsets per-token raise via per-task cost," but **the substantive raise is the core of the user's prediction.** References: [Apidog - GPT-5.5 Pricing](https://apidog.com/blog/gpt-5-5-pricing/)
- **DeepSeek V4 Pro at "7x cheaper than Claude Opus 4.7," released the same day as GPT-5.5** — $1.74 input / $3.48 output per M tokens, Apache 2.0 open weights, Huawei Ascend tight-integration strategy. The **"frontier raise × near-frontier crash" two-pole structure forms completely on a single day.** References: [UCStrategies - DeepSeek V4 Pro Lands on GPT-5.5 Day](https://ucstrategies.com/news/deepseek-v4-pro-lands-on-gpt-5-5-day-1m-context-open-weights-and-seven-times-cheaper-than-claude-opus-4-7/) /  / [OfficeChai - DeepSeek V4-Pro & V4-Flash](https://officechai.com/ai/deepseek-v4-pro-deepseek-v4-flash-benchmarks-pricing/)
- **Structural progress on role split** — DeepSeek V4 Pro is above Claude Sonnet 4.5 on agentic, Opus 4.5-class; IMOAnswerBench 89.8 (above Claude Opus 4.7 75.3); locally / on-prem operatable on open weights, concretizing the **"daily ops on Apache 2.0 open weights, frontier reasoning on cloud APIs"** dividing line. Tencent Hunyuan 3.0 / Qwen 3.5 / Kimi K2.5 / GLM-5 and other Chinese open-weight contenders move the same way. References: [BenchLM - DeepSeek V4 Pro Benchmarks](https://benchlm.ai/models/deepseek-v4-pro) / [Renovate QR - Chinese AI Models in April 2026](https://renovateqr.com/blog/chinese-ai-models-april-2026)
- **Power / compute scarcity side** — Tesla 2026 capex >$25B (Optimus / FSD / chip fab), Giga Texas's second factory at 10M units/year, NVIDIA Q1 FY27 print (May 20) consensus at +77% revenue, Amazon × Anthropic 10-year $100B commit + 5GW Trainium capacity — AI compute / power / capex co-expansion remains the root cause for SaaS price-increase pressure. References:  / [Cloud Computing News - Amazon-Anthropic $25B](https://www.cloudcomputing-news.net/news/amazon-anthropic-25-billion-investment/) / [MediaPost - Tesla Earth Day Optimus](https://www.mediapost.com/publications/article/414491/tesla-plans-earth-day-celebration.html)

The user's "**pricing-system change leads**" hits decisively with GPT-5.5's 2x increase. The "**role split (cloud = high-intelligence / local = daily)**" advances as DeepSeek V4 Pro's Apache 2.0 open weights bring Opus 4.5-class to local hands, sharpening the line. Both moves happen on the same day, making this user prediction the **most accurate prediction of the week**.

### 3. "RL / LLM-based real-world-phenomenon prediction performance improvement broadens"

Today's report has **no direct empirical case** (RL improvement on scheduled-task-style LLM forecasting). Adjacent:

- **Claude Code v2.1.117's `/resume` auto-summary + model persistence strengthening** — `/resume` auto-summarizes stalled / large sessions before reload to prevent context overflow; boot header shows model source. Strengthening the harness's "long-horizon reasoning / persistent context optimization" is the foundation for the prediction's forecasting-perf improvement. References: [Claude Code Changelog](https://claudefa.st/blog/guide/changelog)
- **DeepSeek V4 Pro Hybrid Attention Architecture** — Cuts long-context inference cost to 27% of V3.2; KV cache compressed by 10%; agentic at Opus 4.5 self-rated. Long-context reasoning / prediction strengthening sits at the prediction's perimeter. References: [TechXplore - DeepSeek V4 1M context](https://techxplore.com/news/2026-04-deepseek-v4-million-token-context.html) / [BenchLM - DeepSeek V4 Pro Benchmarks](https://benchlm.ai/models/deepseek-v4-pro)
- **OpenAI GPT-5.5 / Codex output tokens cut ~40%** — Per-task plan / verify efficiency improvement is close to forecasting's auto-evaluation loop strengthening, but no direct improvement on RL-based forecasting-specific benches yet. References: [Apidog - GPT-5.5 Pricing](https://apidog.com/blog/gpt-5-5-pricing/)

The prediction's main-line products / research moves — "running an RL loop on prediction benchmarks to train LLM forecasting" — haven't surfaced today either. Adjacent advances exist in long-context / agentic / harness auto-summary, but they're not direct cases of forecasting-perf improvement.
